article_title: Over 600 House Platforms Identified at Ireland's Brusselstown Ring, Making It the Largest Prehistoric Settlement in Britain or Ireland

content_preview.title: Over 600 House Platforms Identified at Ireland's Brusselstown Ring, Making It the Largest Prehistoric Settlement in Britain or Ireland
content_preview.summary: Queen's University Belfast researchers have identified more than 600 suspected house platforms at a Bronze Age hillfort in County Wicklow, proposing it as Ireland's earliest proto-town, two millennia before the Vikings.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

A study published in the journal *Antiquity* has identified more than 600 suspected prehistoric house platforms within the Brusselstown Ring hillfort in County Wicklow, Ireland, according to [Phys.org](https://phys.org/news/2025-12-scientists-uncovered-evidence-ireland-largest.html). The finding makes it the largest nucleated settlement yet documented anywhere in prehistoric Britain or Ireland, and researchers at Queen's University Belfast have proposed the site as the island's earl...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written archaeology article covering a significant prehistoric discovery with appropriate scholarly context.
editor_notes.source_verification: Irish Times confirms 600+ house platforms, QUB/Brandherm, County Wicklow, proto-town, water cistern, aerial surveys 2017/2022, 98 roundhouses inner + 509 outer. Interesting Engineering confirms 600 platforms, Bronze Age dating, cistern. Phys.org archived.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified. The egalitarian characterization is from the Irish Times. Dating ranges consistent across sources.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. Appropriately frames findings as requiring further excavation.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Irish archaeology or Brusselstown Ring coverage.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Solid archaeology article with verified claims. Approved.
